title: centaurea pectinata var. supina
intro: Centaurea pectinata var. supina, also known as prairie Indian paint, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of centaurea pectinata var. supina include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and improving respiratory function. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it can also be used topically as an ointment or salve to treat various health issues.
